tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for context discovery: catalog full top-level directory structure, system intent (read README, CHANGELOG, ARCHITECTURE.md), tech stack (Python, customtkinter, pytest, ruff, PyInstaller), existing quality signals (tests, linter, CI), and UI/UX presence determination (confirm customtkinter GUI exists). Output all five sections unabbreviated. Source: FR-001, FR-002, FR-013.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns all five sections populated. UI presence flag is set to TRUE (customtkinter confirmed). No prior QA audit results referenced.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for architecture enhancement analysis. Analyze all root-level Python source files (api_server.py, app_gui.py, config.py, rag_engine.py, llm_interface.py, vector_store.py, document_processor.py, engine_factory.py, reranking.py, security.py, auth.py, app_paths.py, main.py, query_transformer.py, utils.py). Evaluate for abstraction opportunities, cohesion improvements, interface clarity, and dependency improvements. Each finding must cite exact file path and quote relevant code. Source: FR-003, FR-004.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns findings in [ARCH-N] format with file paths, current state quotes, enhancement descriptions, impact, and effort estimates. No defect/bug reports — only enhancement opportunities.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for code quality and elegance analysis. Analyze all root-level Python source files and all test files in tests/ (35 test files including integration/ and regression/). Evaluate for readability, idiomatic Python patterns, test quality (behavior vs implementation verification, edge case coverage, setup duplication, assertion specificity), and developer experience (JSDoc/docstrings for public APIs, error message context, magic numbers, naming consistency). Source: FR-005, FR-006.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns findings in [QUAL-N] format. Test quality findings reference specific test files. No defects reported — only enhancement opportunities.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for performance enhancement analysis. Analyze all root-level Python source files for computational efficiency (repeated iteration, missing memoization, N+1 patterns), memory usage (object retention, missing cleanup, data structure mismatches), async patterns (sequential awaits, missing caching, unbounded concurrency), and bundle/startup optimization (PyInstaller bundling, lazy initialization, import optimization). Scope performance expectations to minimum hardware target: 11th gen i5, 16GB RAM, SSD. Source: FR-007, FR-008.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns findings in [PERF-N] format. Bundle/startup findings reference PyInstaller spec files and build scripts. Performance assumptions scoped to i5-11600/16GB/SSD floor.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for resilience and observability enhancement analysis. Analyze all root-level Python source files for error handling (swallowed errors, generic messages, retry opportunities, graceful degradation), logging/observability (missing traces, log quality, structured fields, debug accessibility), and configuration robustness (validation, sensible defaults, sensitive config isolation). Source: FR-009.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns findings in [RES-N] format with file paths and specific code references. No correctness bugs — only resilience improvement opportunities.

tasks.description: Architect gate decision: based on Task 1.1 context discovery confirming customtkinter GUI presence (TRUE), proceed with all six UI/UX analysis passes. This task is the gate — it confirms the conditional branch and logs the decision. If UI were absent, this task would record skip and jump to Phase 3. Source: FR-002, FR-010, FR-013.
tasks.acceptance: UI presence flag confirmed TRUE from Task 1.1 output. Decision logged. All six UI/UX passes authorized to proceed.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_designer for visual hierarchy and layout enhancement pass on the customtkinter desktop GUI (app_gui.py). Evaluate using desktop GUI heuristics (NOT web): primary action visibility on each window, typographic heading hierarchy, whitespace grouping, layout consistency across windows, edge cases with realistic data (long strings, empty states, single-item lists), designed empty states (messaging, guidance, call-to-action). Each finding must cite exact file path and component/class name. Source: FR-010, FR-011.
tasks.acceptance: Designer returns findings in [UI-HIER-N] format with component names, current state, enhancement, user impact, and effort. All heuristics are desktop-native (window layout, widget sizing, system fonts).

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_designer for interaction design and feedback enhancement pass on the customtkinter desktop GUI. Evaluate: interactive element feedback (hover, active, focus states), loading states for async operations (model loading, document processing, query execution), success and error state communication, destructive action confirmations, form validation specificity, interaction flow optimization, transition animations for state changes. Desktop-specific: window close/save prompts, system tray interactions, keyboard shortcuts. Source: FR-010, FR-011.
tasks.acceptance: Designer returns findings in [UI-INT-N] format with component names. All patterns reference desktop GUI interactions (tkinter event bindings, after() callbacks, progress bars, dialog boxes).

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_designer for accessibility and inclusivity enhancement pass on the customtkinter desktop GUI. Evaluate against WCAG 2.2 AA: keyboard-only operability (tab order, mnemonic keys), meaningful alt text for icons/non-text elements, color contrast (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text, 3:1 UI components), form input labeling (not just placeholder text), error message association (aria-describedby equivalent in tkinter), screen reader announcements (state changes), touch target size (44x44px minimum), color-only indicators needing secondary cues. Desktop-specific: Windows High Contrast Mode compatibility, screen magnifier compatibility, narrator support. Source: FR-010, FR-011, FR-012.
tasks.acceptance: Designer returns findings in [UI-A11Y-N] format with WCAG criterion cited per finding. All evaluation uses Windows 11 accessibility context (Narrator, High Contrast, keyboard navigation).

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_designer for typography and visual polish enhancement pass on the customtkinter desktop GUI. Evaluate: consistent type scale, line lengths for readability, line height appropriateness, font weight hierarchy, letter-spacing, consistent semantic element styling, text truncation/overflow handling, color palette consistency, border radii/shadow/spacing consistency. Desktop-specific: system font metrics, DPI scaling behavior, Windows display settings compatibility. Source: FR-010, FR-011.
tasks.acceptance: Designer returns findings in [UI-VIS-N] format with category (Typography/Color/Spacing/Polish). All references use desktop rendering context (CTkLabel, CTkEntry, system font rendering, DPI awareness).

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_explorer for UI performance and perceived performance enhancement pass. Evaluate: component re-render frequency (customtkinter widget update patterns), large list rendering (CTkScrollableFrame performance), inline expensive computations during render, perceived-performance patterns (optimistic updates, progress indicators, progressive disclosure), animation performance (layout animation causing reflow), first meaningful content speed. Desktop-specific: tkinter mainloop responsiveness, thread safety for GUI updates, GIL considerations for CPU-bound work during UI interaction. Scope to minimum hardware: i5-11600/16GB/SSD. Source: FR-010, FR-011.
tasks.acceptance: Explorer returns findings in [UI-PERF-N] format. All performance references scoped to i5-11600/16GB/SSD. Desktop-specific patterns (mainloop blocking, thread safety, GIL) addressed.

tasks.description: Delegate to paid_designer for consistency and design system alignment enhancement pass on the customtkinter desktop GUI. Evaluate: equivalent UI patterns implemented differently across windows/screens, hardcoded style values (colors, spacing, radii) that should use shared constants or theme config, component variants that could share a base, repeated UI patterns extractable into reusable classes, navigation structure consistency, mental model alignment with user task flow. Desktop-specific: customtkinter theming system (CTkTheme), widget appearance consistency, system theme integration (light/dark mode). Source: FR-010, FR-011.
tasks.acceptance: Designer returns findings in [UI-CON-N] format. Findings reference customtkinter theming and widget consistency. Reusable component extraction opportunities identified.

tasks.description: Compile ALL findings from Tasks 1.2–1.5 and 2.1–2.6 into a single structured list. Delegate to paid_critic for validation gate. For each finding, critic must classify as CONFIRMED HIGH-VALUE, CONFIRMED MEDIUM-VALUE, or REJECTED (with reason: false positive, contradicts system intent, stylistic preference, or already handled). Critic must verify file paths exist, verify 'Current State' matches actual code, and identify implementation dependencies between findings. Source: FR-014, FR-015, FR-016, FR-017.
tasks.acceptance: Critic returns full validated list with rejections removed. Each confirmed finding has confidence level and verified file path. Dependency map provided. Rejection reasons stated for each rejected finding.

tasks.description: Pass critic-validated findings from Task 3.1 to paid_reviewer for final report synthesis. Reviewer produces .swarm/enhancement-report.md with: executive summary (2-4 sentences), top 10 highest-impact enhancements ranked, full catalog organized by all applicable categories (ARCH-*, QUAL-*, PERF-*, RES-*, UI-HIER-*, UI-INT-*, UI-A11Y-*, UI-VIS-*, UI-PERF-*, UI-CON-*), implementation roadmap (Phase 1 Quick Wins, Phase 2 Meaningful Improvements, Phase 3 Architectural Investments), and codebase strengths section with specific file/pattern citations. Reviewer must read back the file and verify every section has content and every file path cited is real. Source: FR-018, FR-019, FR-020.
tasks.acceptance: .swarm/enhancement-report.md created with all required sections populated. Top 10 ranked. Roadmap has 3 phases. Codebase strengths cited with file paths. No source files modified. No prior QA audit referenced.

tasks.description: Architect verification: read .swarm/enhancement-report.md, verify all sections present, verify no source files were modified (git status check), verify report does not reference prior QA audit results. Confirm delivery to user. Source: SC-006, SC-007, SC-008.
tasks.acceptance: Report verified: all sections populated, zero source file modifications confirmed, no prior QA audit references found. User notified of delivery.
